subject: Chemistry
solution:
Analyzing lone pairs on central atoms:
HNO₃: N is central with 3 bonds and 0 lone pairs
H₂SO₄: S is central with 4 bonds and 0 lone pairs
NF₃: N is central with 3 bonds and 1 lone pair
O₃: Central O with 2 bonding regions and 1 lone pair
NF₃ has maximum lone pairs (1) with the clearest structure. N undergoes sp³ hybridization with 1 lone pair and 3 bonding pairs giving trigonal pyramidal geometry. The F-N-F bond angle is reduced from ideal tetrahedral 109.5° due to lone pair repulsion, resulting in approximately 102°.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Hybridization in Chemistry is defined as the concept of mixing two atomic orbitals to give rise to a new type of hybridized orbitals. This intermixing usually results in the formation of hybrid orbitals having entirely different energies, shapes, etc. The atomic orbitals of the same energy level mainly take part in hybridization. However, both fully filled and half-filled orbitals can also take part in this process, provided they have equal energy.

Here, BF3 has sp2 hybridisation while BF4- has sp3 hybridisation.

subject: Chemistry
solution: In XeOF2, the central atom has 3 bond pairs and 2 lone pairs. Hence, the electrons will enter a trigonal bipyramidal geometry. The 2 lone pairs will take up equatorial positions giving the molecule a T-shape. Xe in XeOF4 has 5 bond pairs and 1 lone pair. The electrons will take up square bipyramidal geometry, but due to a lone pair, the shape of the molecule will be square pyramidal.

The solvents having an appreciable net dipole moment are known as polar solvents.

CHCl3 is polar due the presence of a net dipole moment. There is a difference in electronegativity of the central atom, carbon, hydrogen, and chlorine. This will create a dipole in the molecule. As a dipole has formed in chloroform molecule, due to electronegativity difference, which makes it polar in nature.

Hence, option D is the answer.

solution:
The pi bonding molecular orbital has a larger electron density above and below the internuclear axis due to the symmetry of the orbital, interference of atomic orbitals, and overlap of atomic orbitals. The electron density is minimum along the internuclear axis due to the nodal plane.

A π bonding molecular orbital has higher electron density above and below inter nuclear axis.

This π* orbital has two nodes: one node is the plane which contains the atoms, and the other node is a plane perpendicular to this, between the two carbon atoms.

solution:
Interionic/intermolecular forces ∝ charge present on species
As ions are charged while dipole is only partially charged so the relative strength of interionic/intermolecular forces in decreasing order is:
Ion-Ion > Ion-Dipole > Dipole-Dipole

solution: In all these molecules O2​ and NO, is stabilized by removal of electrons because it is having electrons in the anti-bonding orbital. And thus removal of electron from the anti bonding orbital, increases the bond order of O2 and NO​. And thus is becomes more stable after removal of electrons. Bond order of O2+​is 2.5 and it is more than that of O2​ which is having bond order as 2. Similarly Bond order of NO is 2.5 and that of NO+ is 3.

In CHCl3, dipole moment of C-H is added to the resultant dipole moment of three C-Cl bonds, hence compound has non-zero value of dipole moment.

Electronegativity of carbon =2.5
Electronegativity of hydrogen=2.1.


Elelctrongegativity of C=2.5
Electronegativity of Cl=3

Due to symmetry and similar bonds, vector summation of net dipole moments of both CH4, CCl4 are zero, hence equal.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The autoionization of bromine trifluoride is given by the reaction:

$2\text{BrF}_3 \rightleftharpoons \text{BrF}_2^{\oplus} + \text{BrF}_4^{\ominus}$

For the cation $\text{BrF}_2^{\oplus}$:
The central atom Br has $7$ valence electrons. Due to the positive charge, it has $7 - 1 = 6$ electrons available.
It forms $2$ single bonds with F atoms, leaving $4$ electrons, which constitute $2$ lone pairs.
Steric number = $2$ (bond pairs) + $2$ (lone pairs) = $4$.
The hybridization is $sp^3$. With $2$ bond pairs and $2$ lone pairs, the shape is bent (or V-shaped).

For the anion $\text{BrF}_4^{\ominus}$:
The central atom Br has $7$ valence electrons. Due to the negative charge, it has $7 + 1 = 8$ electrons available.
It forms $4$ single bonds with F atoms, leaving $4$ electrons, which constitute $2$ lone pairs.
Steric number = $4$ (bond pairs) + $2$ (lone pairs) = $6$.
The hybridization is $sp^3d^2$. To minimize repulsion, the $2$ lone pairs occupy opposite (trans) positions in the octahedral geometry, resulting in a square planar shape.

Thus, the shapes of the cation and anion are bent and square planar, respectively.

Answer: bent, square planar

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Given, magnetic moment of di-oxygen species=1.73 B.M.

We know, magnetic moment = n n+2 , where n=number of unpaired electrons

1.73= n n+2

n=1
So, species should contain one unpaired electron.

Let us check by electronic configuration of each species

O2=σ1s2 σ*1s2 σ2s2 σ*2s2 σ2p2z π2p2x=π2p2y π*2p1x=π*2p1yO2+=σ1s2 σ*1s2 σ2s2 σ*2s2 σ2p2z π2p2x=π2p2y π*2p1x=π*2p0yO2-=σ1s2 σ*1s2 σ2s2 σ*2s2 σ2p2z π2p2x=π2p2y π*2p2x=π*2p1y
 

As clear by above electronic configurations that, O2 has two unpaired electrons and O2- & O2+  Each has only one unpaired electron; hence magnetic moment of  O2+ & O2- is 1.73 B.M.

solution:
The molecular formulas for ammonia and phosphine are NH3 and  PH3
, respectively. As we know, Nitrogen is more electronegative and smaller in size than phosphorus. Therefore, hydrogen bonding occurs in ammonia in comparison to phosphine. Hydrogen bonding is the force of attraction between hydrogen and an electronegative element which is smaller in size. In the case of phosphine, only weak van der waal’s forces of attraction exist between the molecules. Since hydrogen bonding is stronger than weak van der waal’s forces of attraction. Hence, ammonia has a higher boiling point than phosphine.
In all, we can say that hydrogen bonding is responsible for the higher boiling point of ammonia in comparison to phosphine.

N has small atomic size and high electronegativity. Hence, NH3 can form hydrogen bonds. P has large size and low electronegativity. Hence, PH3 cannot form hydrogen bonds.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
2s and 2p atomic orbitals combine, forming σ (sigma) and π (pi) molecular orbitals. σ orbitals result from head-on overlap, while π orbitals arise from sideways overlap. This molecular orbital theory explains the bonding in molecules

 2s orbital will form, two molecular orbitals σ2 s and σ*2 s.
2p orbital will form six molecular orbitals σ2pz and σ*2pz. π2px,π2py and π*2px,π*2py

Hence the total number of molecular orbitals formed from 2s and 2p atomic orbitals of a diatomic molecule formed is 8.
